name: Ampicillin-sulbactam
indications:
## Main indications

Broad-spectrum antibiotic.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Lyophilized powder vial containing 1 g ampicillin + 0,5 g sulbactam (PharmaVial, Drawer, Klonal, Norgreen, Northia**, Richmond).
adulto.administration: Direct IV: Yes. Reconstitute 1,5 g in 3 mL SWFI and administer over 3–5 min. Intermittent IV: Yes. Administer 1,5 g in 100 mL 0.9% NaCl over 15–30 min.
adulto.notes: 5% dextrose accelerates ampicillin hydrolysis (use immediately). Prefer dilution with 0.9% NaCl.

pediatrico.presentation: Powder vial: 1,5 g (ampicillin: 1 g + sulbactam: 0,5 g)
pediatrico.infusionRate: Between 15 and 30 min with continuous infusion pump.
pediatrico.dose: Mild, moderate infections and prophylaxis: Children: 150 mg (ampicillin + sulbactam)/kg/day every 6 h, Adults: 1500 mg/dose every 6 h; maximum dose: 6 g/day (ampicillin + sulbactam). Severe infections (meningitis, bacteremia, complicated intra-abdominal infections, osteomyelitis): Children: 300 mg (ampicillin + sulbactam)/kg/day every 6 h; Adults: 3000 mg/dose every 6 h. Maximum dose: 12 g/day (ampicillin + sulbactam).
pediatrico.notes: In patients with sodium restriction, note that 1500 mg of sultamicillin contains 5 mEq of sodium. Interacts with allopurinol (may increase frequency of ampicillin rash).

neonatal.dose: Dose according to postmenstrual age and weight (NICU protocol).

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 1 h at room temperature and 4 h refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 3 h at room temperature and 24 h refrigerated.

## Pediatric guide

- 8 h at room temperature (25°C). 48 h refrigerated (4°C). Store at room temperature protected from light.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Injection-site pain and phlebitis, diarrhea, rash, nausea, headache, erythema. Mild elevation of hepatic enzymes. Rare: hematologic abnormalities.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — ampicilina + SULBACTAM (code 0903, ATC J01CA).
